title: Aurora Galactica And Platinum Project Guide
strapline: A current, hosted map of the project: what Platinum is, what each game owns, how ingestion and conformance work, how release lanes move, and which maintained docs are the source of truth.
currentGoal: Keep production trustworthy on the refreshed 1.3.0 public family while reviewing the deliberate 1.4.0 candidate family: improve Aurora's Galaga-like gameplay/audio/visual quality, keep Platinum clean as the reusable host, and turn ingestion plus local harnessing into a repeatable path for future games.

cards.title: What Shipped
cards.body: `1.3.0` remains the current public Aurora / Platinum production family. Hosted `/dev` and hosted `/beta` now carry the first deliberate `1.4.0` candidate family as `1.4.0.1` and `1.4.0-beta.1`, while the release path keeps the shell, picker, release dashboards, and hosted docs part of the product.

cards.title: What The Platform Owns
cards.body: Platinum owns the shell, release framing, hosted lanes, pack selection path, shared services, conformance dashboard surfaces, and first-class hosted documentation that explain the platform and release model.

cards.title: What The Application Owns
cards.body: Each game owns its own gameplay truth. Aurora owns scoring, stage flow, challenge cadence, capture and rescue, dual-fighter behavior, enemy behavior, visual/audio identity, and Aurora-specific conformance evidence.

cards.title: Current Focus
cards.body: The main work is now candidate advancement: audio/event feedback, level arc, boss and formation grammar, visual reference grounding, resource economics, and first-class playable/conformance follow-through for Galaxy Guardians and future games.

sections.title: Conformance Entry Points
sections.summary: Shareable lane-relative links for Aurora, Galaxy Guardians, and the combined dashboard view.

links.label: Galaxy Guardians Conformance View
links.detail: Direct hosted conformance view for Galaxy Guardians with Guardians selected on load.

links.label: Aurora Versus Guardians Compare
links.detail: Shared dashboard landing view with both games surfaced at the top and the selector available for deeper drill-down.

sections.title: Conformance Program
sections.summary: How reference evidence becomes measurable software quality.

cards.title: Ingestion
cards.body: Ingestion gathers source footage, clips, manifests, contact sheets, waveforms, event logs, semantic annotations, and confidence notes. It is the evidence front-end for conformance, not a loose design notebook.

cards.title: Harnessing
cards.body: Harnesses run local CPU/browser checks against reference-derived targets: movement, timing, audio, stage shape, boss formation grammar, visual surfaces, pressure windows, and shell behavior.

cards.title: Metrics
cards.body: Metrics include score, confidence, resolution, evidence links, target gaps, user-experience meaning, and cost to move. A 10/10 score means maxed at current scorer resolution, not perfect imitation.

cards.title: Resource Economics
cards.body: Local CPU and browser runs should carry repeated measurement. Codex/OpenAI/model work is tracked as GPU-equivalent when it materially shapes strategy, harnesses, code, or analysis.

sections.title: Release Ladder
sections.summary: How work is expected to move through the system.
bullets: local `localhost` is the active engineering lane and the source of the current candidate.
hosted `/dev` is the shared integration preview and currently carries the visible `1.4.0.1` forward-review increment.
hosted `/beta` is the reviewed `1.4.0-beta.1` candidate lane.
hosted `/production` is the public promise and should move only from an approved hosted `/beta` candidate.
A meaningful `x.y` release should now include a complete documentation pass before hosted `/production` promotion.

sections.title: Platform Versus Applications
sections.summary: The architectural split we need to preserve.

cards.title: Platinum
cards.body: Platinum owns shared shell framing, runtime hosting, pack selection, shared services, release tooling, and the hosted documentation and release surfaces that apply to more than one game.

cards.title: Aurora Galactica
cards.body: Aurora is the first shipped application on Platinum. It owns game rules, challenge and scoring behavior, capture and rescue, and Aurora-specific content and gameplay harnesses.

cards.title: Galaxy Guardians
cards.body: Galaxy Guardians is currently a playable-preview sibling application on Platinum. Its current job is to prove second-game hosting, game-owned identity, and first-class conformance growth without pretending the experience is already fully mature.

sections.title: Automation-First Gating
sections.summary: The release process should prefer automation over memory or manual estimation whenever practical.
bullets: Build and publish preflight should fail if the hosted docs set is incomplete.
Gameplay, shell, and platform boot behavior should be covered by focused harnesses whenever the behavior is stable enough to automate.
Hosted lane verification should confirm live labels and, when relevant, live input behavior.
Manual review should mostly be reserved for feel, visual quality, and presentation clarity where automation is still weaker.

sections.title: Likely Next Steps
sections.summary: The work that most naturally follows the current shipped state.
bullets: Use the conformance dashboard to choose the highest-value Aurora investment by score gap, user impact, confidence, and resource cost.
Prioritize audio/event feedback, level arc, boss/formation grammar, and visual reference grounding for the next major user-experience lift.
Keep Platinum shell and docs discipline boring and reliable while the games become more ambitious.
Advance Galaxy Guardians through ingestion-backed reference evidence before treating it as a serious public playable candidate.
